extraction.one_line_summary: JAKOBSEN Fabio won sprint after LAPORTE Christophe led Jumbo echelon offensive that split peloton twice, dropping GC contenders McNulty and Almeida 2+ minutes
tactical_patterns: Jumbo-Visma and QuickStep jointly drove echelons to fracture race, protecting GC riders while eliminating rival climbers early in stage race.
Second split occurred turning into wind with 8km left, showing breakaway advantage evaporates when peloton accelerates with wind direction change.
LAPORTE's yellow jersey disrupted QuickStep leadout positioning at 1km, yet JAKOBSEN still won showing team sprint superiority overcomes tactical interference.
BISSEGER's late solo attack 8km out failed because LOMBART's single sustained pull neutralized gap better than rotating chase.

rider_signals.MCNULTY Brandon: negative: Lost 2:05 in echelons alongside Almeida, revealing positional weakness when wind splits occur midway through short stage race
rider_signals.ALMEIDA João: negative: Caught out in same split as McNulty, suggesting UAE team cohesion failed when Jumbo/QuickStep accelerated
rider_signals.SCHACHMANN Maximilian: negative: Defending champion lost 2:05 in echelons, indicating form deficit or poor team support structure
rider_signals.YATES Simon: neutral: Initially distanced in first split but rejoined, showing recovery ability but concerning initial positioning
rider_signals.VLASOV Aleksandr: positive: Named alongside Roglič as GC contender safely navigating both splits, elevating his status this race
rider_signals.LOMBART Yves: positive: Single massive pull caught Bisseger and set up Jakobsen, demonstrating exceptional power reserves after echelon work
rider_signals.LAPORTE Christophe: positive: Maintained yellow while actively disrupting rival sprint trains at 1km, dual-role execution rare in GC leaders
